new_classes.definition_en: Natural or artificial mineral water and carbonated (aerated) water, not containing added sugar or other sweetening matter nor flavoured, classified under CN subheading 2201 10 (Chapter 22). Excludes natural mineral water presented in spray cans for skin care (heading 3304).

coverage_explanation: The legal text covers: (1) mineral water and carbonated/aerated water under subheadings 2201 10 11–2201 10 90 — captured by the new MineralWater class with cnHeadingCode '220110' and the isCarbonated restriction; (2) the positive scope reference to HS Explanatory Notes 2201 B and C (natural mineral water, artificial mineral water, aerated water) — captured at the class level via the definition; (3) the explicit exclusion of natural mineral water in spray cans for skin care (heading 3304) — expressed in the class definition but cannot be fully formalised as an OWL complement restriction without a dedicated 'SkinCareSpray' class in the TBox, resulting in a partial coverage gap; (4) the specific 8-digit code 22011011 is a TARIC-level subdivision within the 2201 10 range — the isCarbonated hasValue 'true' approximates this but the precise intra-subheading split between 22011011 and 22011090 requires additional criteria (e.g. natural vs. artificial) not yet modelled, hence coverage is not 1.0.
